1.notes: Opening par 5 plays downhill with a slight dogleg left. Willie Park Jr. design features native oaks lining both sides. Creek runs along left side from 400 yards out.

2.notes: Long uphill par 4 at 471 yards from the tips. Fairway bunkers pinch the landing zone. Elevated green typical of Willie Park Jr.

3.notes: Downhill par 4 with slight right-to-left movement. At 461 yards, this is one of the most challenging holes. Hosted pivotal moments in the 2003 US Open.

4.notes: 400-yard par 4 with tree-lined fairway. Traditional design demands accuracy off the tee.

5.notes: Uphill par 4 at 417 yards. Slight left bend requires positioning. Native oaks frame the approach.

6.notes: 187-yard par 3 playing downhill. Tiered green with bunkers surrounding. Club selection is critical.

7.notes: 430-yard par 4 dogleg right. Uphill approach to a challenging kidney-shaped green. Strategic bunkering at the corner.

8.notes: Uphill par 3 at 247 yards. One of the longest par 3s on tour venues. Water behind the green penalizes over-clubbing.

9.notes: Downhill finishing hole on the front at 445 yards. Slight left movement requires careful positioning off the tee.

10.notes: Uphill par 4 at 444 yards opens the back nine. Typical Olympia Fields challenge with tree-lined fairway and strategic bunkering.

11.notes: 402-yard par 4 with slight right movement. Greenside bunkers guard both sides of the putting surface.

12.notes: 389-yard par 4 dogleg left. Butterfield Creek comes into play on the left side. Downhill approach to protected green.

13.notes: 168-yard uphill par 3. Tiered green with bunkers front and back. Wind can significantly affect club selection.

14.notes: 444-yard par 4 with slight left movement. One of the toughest holes on the course with SI of 2.

15.notes: 576-yard par 5 dogleg left. Massive elevation drop from tee to green. Creek runs along left side. Major scoring opportunity for long hitters.

16.notes: 215-yard uphill par 3 with water short of the green. Club selection is crucial. One of the most demanding par 3s.

17.notes: 455-yard par 4 dogleg right. Penultimate hole demands accuracy. Fairway bunker guards the corner.

18.notes: 496-yard uphill finishing par 4. Iconic conclusion with the Tudor clubhouse in the background. Fairway bunkers guard both sides of the landing zone. Jon Rahm famously won the 2020 BMW Championship here.

1.notes: 408-yard opening par 4. Desert waste areas line both sides. Bobby Weed design features arroyos throughout. Bentgrass greens.

2.notes: 469-yard par 4 with slight right movement. Desert wash runs along the left. One of the harder holes on the front.

3.notes: 492-yard par 5. Reachable for long hitters but water short of green demands precision. Featured hole on the Shriners Open.

4.notes: 450-yard par 4 with slight left movement. Stroke index 3 - one of the more demanding holes on the course.

5.notes: 197-yard downhill par 3. Green surrounded by bunkers. Desert landscape with mountain views.

6.notes: 430-yard par 4 dogleg right. Stroke index 1 - most difficult hole on the course. Desert wash guards the right side.

7.notes: 382-yard par 4. Shorter hole but narrow fairway demands accuracy. Good birdie opportunity.

8.notes: 239-yard downhill par 3. Long and challenging with bunkers surrounding the green. Tiered putting surface.

9.notes: 563-yard par 5 dogleg left. Reachable in two but water guards the front of the green. Major scoring swing potential.

10.notes: 420-yard par 4 to start the back nine. Desert waste area on left demands accuracy off the tee.

11.notes: 448-yard par 4 with slight left movement. Stroke index 2 - demanding second shot to well-protected green.

12.notes: 442-yard par 4. Desert waste areas on both sides create a demanding driving corridor.

13.notes: 606-yard par 5. Longest hole on the course. Reachable for bombers but water protects the green. Slight right movement.

14.notes: 168-yard downhill par 3. Featured hole with lake guarding the left side. Bunkers right. Par is a good score.

15.notes: 341-yard drivable par 4. Featured hole. Five bunkers surround the severely undulated green. Eagle or bogey potential.

16.notes: 560-yard par 5 dogleg left. Reachable but lake left of green demands precision. Featured hole on Shriners broadcast.

17.notes: 196-yard par 3. Featured hole with lake guarding the left side. Downhill with wind affecting club selection.

18.notes: 444-yard finishing par 4. Featured hole. Dogleg left with lake protecting the green on the left. Aggressive second shot can lead to birdie or bogey. Very deep green.

1.notes: Opening par 4 with directional bunkering typical of Rees Jones renovation. Original RTJ routing preserved. Target green with accessible miss areas.

2.notes: Par 5 with slight right movement. Uphill approach to tiered green. Reachable for long hitters. RTJ routing with Rees Jones bunkering.

3.notes: Downhill par 3 with bunkers surrounding the green. Visible hazards allow for strategic play.

4.notes: Dogleg left par 4. Directional bunkering guides the tee shot. Classic RTJ strategic design with Rees Jones updates.

5.notes: Uphill par 4 with target green. Bunkers guard both sides of the putting surface.

6.notes: Downhill par 5 with water left of the green. Reachable in two but risk-reward approach. NCAA Championship host course.

7.notes: Par 4 requiring accuracy. Directional bunker left guides the tee shot.

8.notes: Uphill par 3 to tiered green. Bunkers front and back require precise distance control.

9.notes: Downhill dogleg right to finish the front nine. Fairway bunker at the corner. Challenging approach to kidney green.

10.notes: Uphill par 4 to start the back nine. Fairway bunkers pinch the landing zone. Tiered green demands precise approach.

11.notes: Par 5 with slight left movement. Water guards the front of the green. Risk-reward second shot for those going for it in two.

12.notes: Downhill par 3 with water left. Beautiful but demanding. Club selection crucial.

13.notes: Uphill dogleg right. Demanding approach to kidney-shaped green. Fairway bunker guards the corner.

14.notes: Par 4 with tiered green. Fairway bunker left demands accuracy. Rees Jones bunkering renovation 2021.

15.notes: Downhill par 5 with slight right movement. Scoring opportunity. Fairway bunkers frame the second shot landing zone.

16.notes: Uphill par 3 with water behind the green. Bunkers front guard against bailouts. Demanding tee shot.

17.notes: Dogleg left par 4 with water on left side. Penultimate hole demands precision. Risk-reward tee shot over the corner.

18.notes: Uphill finishing par 5. Slight right movement with fairway bunkers defining both sides of the second shot landing area. Strong finish to a championship layout. Home of Duke Blue Devils golf.
